project.name: PropManager Native Encrypted Messaging
project.description: Signal Protocol E2E encrypted messaging system for secure tenant-landlord communication within the ticketing and complaints workflow. Provides zero-knowledge architecture where the server cannot read message contents, with forward secrecy and post-compromise security.

included: E2E encrypted messaging within ticket context
Real-time message delivery via Laravel Reverb

qualityStandards.description: This is a security-critical system. Every cryptographic operation must be implemented correctly. A single bug can compromise all user messages. Build for paranoid security while maintaining excellent UX.
principles: Write failing test FIRST (RED-GREEN-REFACTOR) - especially critical for cryptographic operations
Zero-knowledge architecture: Server NEVER sees plaintext messages or private keys
Forward secrecy: Compromised keys cannot decrypt past messages
Post-compromise security: Sessions heal automatically after breach
Client-side key generation: All keypairs generated in browser, never transmitted
Cryptographic audit trail: Log key operations without exposing secrets
Graceful degradation: Handle key loss, device changes, session corruption
GDPR compliance: Users can export and delete their cryptographic identity
Accessibility: Encryption should be invisible to users—just works
Use FormRequest for validation on all API endpoints
Use Policy for authorization (KeyPolicy, ConversationPolicy, MessagePolicy)
securityRequirements: All private keys stored encrypted in IndexedDB with user-derived key
Session state encrypted before server storage
No plaintext in server logs, error messages, or database
Rate limiting on key bundle requests to prevent enumeration
Signature verification on all prekeys before use
Constant-time comparison for all cryptographic operations

signalProtocol.overview: The Signal Protocol provides end-to-end encryption with forward secrecy and post-compromise security. It combines the X3DH key agreement protocol for initial key exchange with the Double Ratchet algorithm for ongoing message encryption.

x3dh.name: Extended Triple Diffie-Hellman
x3dh.purpose: Establish shared secret between two parties who have never communicated

identityKey.storage: Private in IndexedDB, public on server

signedPreKey.storage: Private in IndexedDB, public+signature on server

oneTimePreKeys.purpose: Ephemeral keys for perfect forward secrecy
oneTimePreKeys.storage: Private in IndexedDB, public on server

keyBundle.description: Public keys fetched before initiating conversation

doubleRatchet.name: Double Ratchet Algorithm
doubleRatchet.purpose: Derive unique encryption key for each message with forward secrecy

dhRatchet.operation: New ephemeral X25519 keypair, DH with recipient's ratchet key

symmetricRatchet.purpose: Forward secrecy—each message has unique key

keyTypes.rootKey: 32 bytes, derived from X3DH, updated on DH ratchet
keyTypes.messageKey: 32 bytes, unique per message, deleted after use

outOfOrder.description: Messages may arrive out of order; store skipped message keys

feedbackLoops.description: Run ALL feedback loops before committing. Do NOT commit if any fail.

commands.typecheck: npm run build -- --mode development 2>&1 | grep -i error

before_commit: No new inline validation in controllers

workflow.description: Standard workflow for implementing each PRD item

steps.name: Write Failing Test
steps.description: Before writing any implementation code, create test(s) that verify the acceptance criteria. For crypto operations, test edge cases extensively.
steps.example: test('user can generate identity keypair', function() { ... expectation that fails ... })

steps.name: Minimal Implementation
steps.description: Write the minimum code to make the test pass. Do not optimize prematurely.

steps.name: Refactor
steps.description: Clean up the implementation while keeping tests green.

steps.name: Verify
steps.description: Run full test suite and quality checks before marking item complete.

key_management.description: Cryptographic key generation, storage, exchange, and rotation

session.description: Double Ratchet session initialization and state management

realtime.description: Real-time delivery, typing indicators, and read receipts

attachments.description: Encrypted file attachments with symmetric key sharing

ui.description: Frontend components for ticket conversation interface

security.description: Key rotation, session healing, and security auditing

tables.name: user_identity_keys
tables.description: Stores public identity keys for each user

columns.name: key_id
columns.description: First 8 bytes of SHA256(public_key), hex encoded

tables.name: user_one_time_prekeys
tables.description: Ephemeral one-time prekeys, consumed on first use

tables.name: ticket_conversations
tables.description: Links tickets to encrypted conversation threads

columns.name: file_path
columns.description: Path to encrypted file in storage

tables.name: message_keys_skipped
tables.description: Skipped message keys for out-of-order message handling

columns.name: ratchet_key_hash
columns.description: SHA256 of DH ratchet public key

tables.name: key_rotation_logs
tables.description: Audit trail for key operations (GDPR compliance)

apiEndpoints.description: Upload user's public identity key (first-time setup)

request.public_key: base64 encoded Ed25519 public key (32 bytes)

apiEndpoints.description: Upload signed prekey and batch of one-time prekeys
Fetch recipient's key bundle for X3DH
apiEndpoints.notes: One-time prekey is consumed and removed from pool

apiEndpoints.description: Get count of remaining one-time prekeys for replenishment check
Send encrypted message within ticket conversation
Get encrypted messages for ticket conversation

realtimeEvents.name: PrekeysLow
realtimeEvents.description: Triggered when one-time prekeys drop below threshold (20)

frontendComponents.name: TicketConversation.vue
frontendComponents.description: Main chat interface embedded in ticket detail page

frontendComponents.name: MessageBubble.vue
frontendComponents.description: Individual message display with sender alignment

frontendComponents.name: EncryptionIndicator.vue
frontendComponents.description: Lock icon showing encryption status and verification option

frontendComponents.name: TypingIndicator.vue
frontendComponents.description: Animated dots showing user is typing

items.title: Generate User Identity Keys on Registration
items.description: When a user registers or first accesses secure messaging, generate an Ed25519 identity keypair client-side and upload the public key to the server.
steps: Create KeySetup.vue component shown on first secure messaging access
Generate Ed25519 keypair using libsodium crypto_sign_keypair()
Store private key encrypted in IndexedDB using user's password-derived key
POST public key to /api/v1/keys/identity
Store key_id in localStorage for quick lookup
Add identity_key_uploaded_at column to users table
Show success confirmation with safety number preview
acceptance_criteria: Identity keypair generated entirely client-side (server never sees private key)
Private key encrypted before IndexedDB storage
Public key successfully uploaded to server
Key generation takes < 500ms on modern devices
User can see their key fingerprint (safety number)
items.verification: Generate keys; verify only public key in server DB; verify private key in IndexedDB is encrypted

items.title: Prekey Bundle Generation and Upload
items.description: Generate and upload a signed prekey and batch of one-time prekeys. The signed prekey is valid for 7 days; one-time prekeys are single-use.
steps: Generate X25519 signed prekey with crypto_box_keypair()
Sign the prekey public key with identity private key
Generate 100 X25519 one-time prekeys
Store all private keys encrypted in IndexedDB
POST prekey bundle to /api/v1/keys/prekeys
Set up periodic check for prekey count replenishment
Listen for PrekeysLow WebSocket event to trigger replenishment
acceptance_criteria: Signed prekey includes valid Ed25519 signature
Server validates signature before storing prekey
100 one-time prekeys generated and uploaded
Prekey replenishment triggers when count < 20
Private prekeys never leave the client
items.verification: Generate prekeys; verify signature validation works; consume prekeys and verify replenishment

items.title: Fetch Recipient's Key Bundle
items.description: Before initiating a conversation, fetch the recipient's public key bundle for X3DH key agreement.
steps: Create getKeyBundle() function in useSignalProtocol.ts
GET /api/v1/keys/{userId}/bundle with bearer auth
Verify signed prekey signature using identity public key
Cache bundle in memory for session duration (don't persist)
Handle case where no one-time prekeys available (X3DH still works)
Rate limit bundle requests to prevent enumeration attacks
acceptance_criteria: Key bundle fetched successfully via API
Signed prekey signature verified client-side
One-time prekey marked as consumed server-side
Graceful fallback when no one-time prekeys available
Rate limiting prevents abuse (max 10 requests/minute)
items.verification: Fetch bundle; verify one-time prekey consumed; fetch again and get different prekey

items.title: Initialize Double Ratchet Session
items.description: Perform X3DH key agreement to establish shared secret, then initialize Double Ratchet session for ongoing encryption.
steps: Implement performX3DH() function computing 3 or 4 DH operations
Derive shared secret using HKDF with protocol-specific info string
Initialize Double Ratchet state with shared secret
Set root key, sending/receiving chain keys
Generate initial ratchet keypair for initiator
Mark session as 'initiator' or 'responder' for ratchet direction

x3dhCalculation.initiator: SK = HKDF(DH(IK_A, SPK_B) || DH(EK_A, IK_B) || DH(EK_A, SPK_B) || DH(EK_A, OPK_B))

acceptance_criteria: X3DH produces identical shared secret on both sides
Double Ratchet initialized with correct root key
Session state can be serialized and restored
Works with and without one-time prekey
Info string includes protocol version for future upgrades
items.verification: Initialize session between two users; verify both derive same root key

items.title: Session State Persistence
items.description: Persist encrypted Double Ratchet session state to both IndexedDB (client) and server for cross-device continuity.
steps: Serialize session state to JSON (root key, chain keys, counters, skipped keys)
Encrypt serialized state with user's storage key
Store in IndexedDB for offline access
Sync encrypted state to server via /api/v1/sessions/sync
Implement version number for conflict resolution
Handle session restore on page reload and device switch
acceptance_criteria: Session state encrypted before server storage
Session restored correctly after page reload
Version conflict detected and latest version used
Conversation works after browser clear (fetch from server)
Deleted sessions cannot be recovered by server admin
items.verification: Send messages; close browser; reopen and continue conversation without re-keying

items.title: Encrypt and Send Message
items.description: Encrypt plaintext message using Double Ratchet and send to recipient through ticket conversation.
steps: Implement ratchetEncrypt(session, plaintext) function
Perform symmetric ratchet step to derive message key
Encrypt plaintext with XChaCha20-Poly1305 using message key
Include header: ratchet public key, previous chain length, message number
POST encrypted message to /api/v1/conversations/{ticketId}/messages
Update and persist session state
Show optimistic UI update while awaiting confirmation
acceptance_criteria: Message encrypted with unique key (never reused)
Header allows recipient to derive same key
Message stored as ciphertext on server (not readable)
Session state updated correctly after send
Optimistic UI shows message before server confirmation
items.verification: Send message; verify server only has ciphertext; verify recipient can decrypt

items.title: Receive and Decrypt Message
items.description: Receive encrypted message via WebSocket and decrypt using Double Ratchet.
steps: Listen for SecureMessageSent event on private-user.{userId} channel
Parse header to get ratchet public key and message numbers
Check if DH ratchet step needed (new ratchet key from sender)
Perform symmetric ratchet steps to derive message key
Decrypt ciphertext with XChaCha20-Poly1305
Update and persist session state
Display decrypted message in UI
Send delivery confirmation
acceptance_criteria: Real-time message reception via WebSocket
Correct DH ratchet step on direction change
Message decrypted and displayed correctly
Session state matches sender's state
Delivery confirmation sent after decryption
items.verification: Send message from user A; verify user B receives and decrypts in < 100ms

items.title: Handle Out-of-Order Messages
items.description: Messages may arrive out of order due to network conditions. Store skipped message keys to decrypt late-arriving messages.
steps: When receiving message with higher number than expected, store skipped keys
Store skipped keys in message_keys_skipped table (encrypted)
Set max skip limit (1000) to prevent DoS
When message arrives, check skipped keys first
Delete skipped key after successful decryption
Set expiration on skipped keys (7 days) for cleanup
acceptance_criteria: Messages decrypt correctly regardless of arrival order
Skipped keys stored encrypted
Max skip limit enforced (reject if would skip > 1000)
Skipped keys cleaned up after use or expiration
No duplicate key storage for same message number
items.verification: Send 10 messages; deliver in random order; all decrypt correctly

items.title: Real-time Message Delivery via Reverb
items.description: Use Laravel Reverb WebSocket for instant message delivery without polling.
steps: Create SecureMessageSent event implementing ShouldBroadcast
Broadcast to private-user.{recipientId} channel
Include only ciphertext and header in broadcast (no plaintext ever)
Add Echo listener in TicketConversation.vue
Handle reconnection and missed message recovery
Fallback to polling if WebSocket unavailable
acceptance_criteria: Messages delivered in < 100ms via WebSocket
Reconnection automatically recovers missed messages
Fallback polling works if WebSocket fails
No plaintext in WebSocket payload
Private channel authorization works correctly
items.verification: Measure delivery latency; disconnect and reconnect; verify no messages lost

items.title: Typing Indicators
items.description: Show 'User is typing...' indicator in real-time while the other party types.
steps: Create UserTyping event for presence channel
Broadcast to private-conversation.{conversationId} channel
Debounce typing detection (send after 500ms of typing, clear after 3s idle)
Add TypingIndicator.vue component with animated dots
Handle multiple participants typing simultaneously
acceptance_criteria: Typing indicator appears within 500ms of typing start
Indicator disappears 3 seconds after typing stops
Multiple typers shown correctly
No typing indicator spam (debounced)
Indicator hidden when own user is typing
items.verification: Type in one browser; see indicator in other browser; stop typing and see indicator disappear

items.title: Read Receipts
items.description: Show double checkmarks when messages have been read by recipient.
steps: Mark message as read when scrolled into view (IntersectionObserver)
PUT /api/v1/messages/{id}/read to update server
Broadcast MessageRead event to sender
Update message UI to show double checkmarks
Batch read receipts for efficiency (every 1 second)
acceptance_criteria: Read receipt sent when message visible for 1 second
Sender sees double checkmarks update in real-time
Batch processing prevents receipt spam
Works offline (syncs when reconnected)
Read timestamps stored for analytics
items.verification: Send message; scroll into view on recipient; verify sender sees read status

items.title: Encrypted File Attachments
items.description: Allow sending encrypted file attachments (images, documents) within ticket conversations.
steps: Generate random 256-bit symmetric key for file
Encrypt file client-side with XChaCha20-Poly1305
Encrypt file key with recipient's current message key
Upload encrypted file to /api/v1/attachments
Send message with attachment reference and encrypted file key
Recipient decrypts file key with message key, then decrypts file
acceptance_criteria: File encrypted entirely client-side before upload
Server stores only encrypted blob (cannot view)
Recipient can download and decrypt file
Image thumbnails generated client-side
File size and type validation before upload
items.verification: Upload image; verify server file is encrypted; download and view on recipient

items.title: Ticket Conversation Thread UI
items.description: Create the main conversation UI embedded in the ticket detail page, separate from ticket comments.
steps: Add TicketConversation.vue component to Tickets/Show.vue
Show encryption indicator and verification option
Display message list with infinite scroll pagination
Auto-scroll to newest message on load and new message
Add message input with send button and attachment button
Show connection status indicator (WebSocket state)
Handle first-time key setup flow if no keys exist

uiGuidelines.placement: Collapsible panel or tab in ticket detail view
uiGuidelines.separateFrom: Ticket comments (comments remain for caretaker visibility)
uiGuidelines.indicator: Green lock icon when encrypted, with 'Verify' option

acceptance_criteria: Conversation panel visible on ticket detail page
Clear visual distinction from ticket comments
Encryption status always visible
Key setup prompts if no identity key exists
Mobile-responsive design
Accessible (keyboard navigation, screen reader)
items.verification: Open ticket; see conversation panel; send and receive messages; verify encryption indicator

items.title: Key Rotation and Session Healing
items.description: Implement key rotation policies and session recovery after key compromise or device loss.
steps: Rotate signed prekey every 7 days automatically
Detect when recipient's identity key changes (potential compromise)
Show security warning when identity key changes
Allow user to verify new key via safety number comparison
Implement session reset that re-establishes X3DH
Log all key rotations for audit compliance
acceptance_criteria: Signed prekeys rotate automatically every 7 days
Identity key change triggers security warning
Safety number matches on both devices when verified
Session can be reset without losing access to old messages
All key operations logged for compliance audit
items.verification: Rotate prekey; verify session continues; change identity key; verify warning shown

implementationPhases.name: Core Messaging
implementationPhases.description: Basic encrypted messaging with real-time delivery

implementationPhases.description: Rich messaging features and polished UI
deliverables: Full conversation UI in ticket page

implementationPhases.name: Security Hardening
implementationPhases.description: Key rotation and advanced security features
